authority: This registry is the machine-readable index of canonical constitutional authority. Where implementation, legacy documents, or derivative materials conflict with this registry, the registry controls. Where this registry conflicts with WOS-CONST-3.5, WOS-CONST-3.5 controls.
registry_role: Machine-readable canonical index, not constitutional amendment authority
registry_controls: Derivative documents, implementation labels, evidence mappings, and legacy references
registry_sha256_method: SHA-256 over json.dumps(registry, sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':'), ensure_ascii=True) with registry_sha256 field excluded

WOS-STATE-1.1.title: Current State of the Sovereign Planes
WOS-STATE-1.1.note: v1.2 pending: Plane I and Plane IV promotions from RescueChain / transition-conductor reproduction session

GUARDIANS-6.2.title: Guardians of the Kernel
GUARDIANS-6.2.role: Meta-governance: Guardian Council, Kernel Change Protocol

WOS-CONST-3.5.title: Constitutional Development Directive
WOS-CONST-3.5.role: Supreme constitutional authority: rules, lifecycle, claim discipline, invariants, objectives, conformance standard, amendment procedure. Status: Constitutionally Frozen.

deprecated_lineages.effect: Historical record only. Must carry deprecation notices if publicly accessible (WOS-CONST-3.1 Rule 6).

items.reason: Superseded branches; violate Constitutional Allocation Rule retroactively

items.lineage: 2025 investor whitepapers / Series A claims (valuation, ARR, pilot counts, user counts)
items.reason: Legacy marketing narrative; no current evidentiary basis; violates Claim Discipline if presented as current

items.lineage: Master Module Index v4.1 (239 modules) and all module-count-led positioning

sovereign_planes.note: Planes are constitutionally Stable by definition. Implementation status per WOS-STATE (v1.1 shown; v1.2 promotions flagged).

planes.name: Constitutional Intelligence
planes.status_note: Promoted from Aspirational: multi-conductor decision generation (art_command, rescuechain) implemented, executed, deterministically reproduced 2026-07-03

planes.name: Physical Systems & Infrastructure
planes.status_note: Simulation responsibility only -- transition_conductor 60-trial Monte Carlo, 0 crashes, reproduced. Hardware remains Aspirational.

planes.name: Verification & Truth
planes.status_note: Promotion blocked on: benchmarked verification accuracy + measured verifier independence

sovereign_planes.evidence_boundary_rule: Plane II owns knowledge provenance (what the system knows and where it came from). Plane VIII owns decision provenance (what the system did and who authorized it). WOS-CONST-3.1 Rule 4.

invariant_registry.authority_ruling: Invariant 23 is Meta-Governance Completeness per Guardians of the Kernel. Flow-Preserving Governance is a design heuristic, NOT an invariant -- its own addendum states it modifies no invariants. This ruling resolves the corpus's principal numbering conflict.

enforcement_legend.ENFORCED: Runtime-enforced in the reference kernel with passing tests
enforcement_legend.SPEC_ONLY: Specification exists; no runtime enforcement located

invariants.name: Meta-Governance Completeness
invariants.note: Guardian Council + KCP defined (GUARDIANS-6.2); shared runtime validator incomplete

invariant_registry.registry_note: Exact per-invariant numbering below n=23 varies across deprecated documents; this registry's numbering is now canonical. Enforcement labels must be revalidated against WOS-KERNEL-3.4 test output before external publication -- treat labels here as best current assessment, not benchmark evidence.

kernel.status: Reference implementation. v3.5 (app_v35.py, test_v35.py, 70 integration tests) is the integration branch.
kernel.rule: All other kernel files (v1.1 through v3.3, cornerstone variants) are historical branches.

ledger_families.ruling: Two distinct ledger primitives exist and must never be conflated in claims.

enterprise.model: Per-record Ed25519 signatures + prev_hash chain + single-writer enforcement
enterprise.properties: Non-repudiation at record level; strongest evidence primitive
enterprise.benchmark: stress_report.json -- 10,000 writes, chain_valid true, 0 errors, ~1253.8 writes/sec (internal, single run)

forensic_demo.model: SHA-256 hash chain + Ed25519 signature over chain head
forensic_demo.properties: Tamper-evident and independently verifiable; chain-head (not per-record) non-repudiation
forensic_demo.reproduction: art_mission_ledger.json and omni_scout_decision_ledger.json deterministically regenerated in clean environment 2026-07-03; verified by from-scratch third-party verifier (independent_verify.py)

scale.E2.5: Reproduced from artifacts in clean environment without author assistance (AI-executed; not arms-length human)

ceiling_holders: governance_ledger.py + art_command.py (tornado scenario): deterministic reproduction, matching sensor hashes and chain heads
transition_conductor.py: 60-trial Monte Carlo reproduced -- 25 complete / 35 typed safe refusals / 0 crashes; chain head match
evidence_maturity.path_to_E3: Human third party (candidate: Bob Ladrach) runs: (1) python3 art_command.py, (2) python3 rescuechain.py, (3) python3 independent_verify.py <ledgers> -- from repo artifacts only, no founder assistance.

open_findings.finding: Medic vote field conflates veto authority with veto exercise ('veto': true on authorizations)
open_findings.fix: Split into veto_authority (unit property) and veto_exercised (per-decision)

Formal tests archive did not reproduce cleanly in external environment (missing pynacl)
open_findings.classification: Documentation Improvement -- pin dependencies, one-command runner

open_findings.finding: Stress benchmark is a single internal run; needs pinned environment, repeated trials, raw logs
Public positioning language predates WOS-CONST-3.1 Canonical External Statement
open_findings.classification: Documentation Improvement -- constitutional debt, highest priority

amendment_log_pointer: See WOS-CONST-3.5 Amendment Log (Amendments 3.0 through 3.5). Changes to this registry require the same procedure.

registry_changelog.change: Supreme authority updated to WOS-CONST-3.5 (Constitutionally Frozen); compatibility floor raised; no substantive classification changes
Added registry_uuid, schema_version, compatibility block, canonical citation format, detached-style registry_sha256 fingerprint (computed excluding the hash field itself)
Authority hierarchy corrected: WOS-CONST-3.1 supreme, registry is index. Added supreme_authority, registry_role, registry_controls fields. ASCII-safe encoding.
